records.title: Citizen service number (BSN)
records.description: The citizen service number is the unique personal number used in contacts with Dutch public authorities. It is assigned automatically when a person is registered in the Personal Records Database.
records.ai_summary: Registering in the BRP normally creates a BSN automatically. The number is then used to identify the person across government, healthcare and education services.

records.title: Personal Records Database registration (BRP)
records.description: The Personal Records Database contains the official personal details of residents and non-residents connected to the Netherlands. Municipalities maintain resident records and register important changes such as an address move.
records.ai_summary: People staying in the Netherlands for the longer term register with their municipality. The BRP record supports a BSN and keeps official address and civil-status information available to authorised organisations.

records.title: Apply for a DigiD
records.description: DigiD is the personal digital identity used to arrange matters online with Dutch government bodies and participating healthcare, education and pension organisations. A standard application requires a BSN, a municipal registration address and a mobile phone.
records.ai_summary: Apply through the official DigiD form using your BSN and registered address. An activation letter is sent to the address recorded by the municipality, after which the account must be activated.

records.title: MijnOverheid and Berichtenbox
records.description: MijnOverheid provides access to personal information and digital correspondence from participating Dutch public authorities. Its Berichtenbox is the secure mailbox used for messages about matters such as taxes, benefits and pensions.

official_source.title: Welke informatie kan ik vinden op MijnOverheid?

records.ai_summary: Activate MijnOverheid and use DigiD to view personal government information and messages. Notification preferences determine which participating organisations send correspondence to the Berichtenbox.

records.title: Dutch passport or identity card
records.description: Dutch citizens apply for a passport or identity card through the municipality where they are registered. Applicants must appear in person, provide an acceptable colour photograph and present their current identity documents.

official_source.title: Passports, identity cards and Dutch citizenship certificates

records.ai_summary: A Dutch passport and identity card prove identity and can be used for travel under their applicable rules. Applications in the Netherlands are handled by the registered municipality and require an in-person appointment.

records.title: Residence permits
records.description: The residence permit required for a stay in the Netherlands depends on the applicant's purpose and circumstances. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service publishes the available permit routes and the requirements for each application.
records.ai_summary: Select the IND permit route that matches the purpose of stay, such as work, study or family. Requirements, required documents, sponsorship and the application procedure differ between routes.

records.title: Healthcare benefit (zorgtoeslag)
records.description: Healthcare benefit is a government contribution towards the cost of Dutch health insurance. Eligibility depends on factors including age, having qualifying Dutch health insurance, income and assets.
records.ai_summary: Check the current income, asset, age and insurance conditions before applying. The allowance is managed through Mijn Toeslagen, and changes in income or household circumstances should be reported promptly.

records.title: Housing benefit (huurtoeslag)
records.description: Housing benefit is a contribution towards rent for eligible tenants. The amount and eligibility depend on current rules and personal circumstances such as the home, rent, household, income and assets.
records.ai_summary: Use the official eligibility check and trial calculation because housing-benefit rules and amounts can change. Applications are submitted through Mijn Toeslagen with DigiD and remain valid while the conditions continue to be met.

records.title: Childcare benefit (kinderopvangtoeslag)
records.description: Childcare benefit contributes to eligible childcare costs. The official service explains eligibility, applications, approved childcare, payment calculations and changes that must be reported.
records.ai_summary: Confirm that the childcare provider and location are approved, then check the current work, study and household conditions. Keep hours, costs, income and family changes current in Mijn Toeslagen.

records.title: File an income tax return
records.description: Dutch income tax returns are generally filed digitally with the Tax Administration. The official guidance distinguishes resident and non-resident situations and explains the available filing routes.
records.ai_summary: Determine whether the resident or non-resident filing route applies and follow the current Tax Administration instructions. DigiD and Mijn Belastingdienst are commonly used for online filing and correspondence.

records.title: Dutch citizenship through naturalisation
records.description: Eligible adults can apply for Dutch nationality through naturalisation at their municipality. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service assesses the application and publishes the current requirements, documents, costs and procedure.

official_source.title: Becoming a Dutch national through naturalisation

records.ai_summary: Check the latest IND requirements before applying through the municipality. A positive decision alone is not the final step: the applicant must attend the naturalisation ceremony and make the declaration of solidarity.

records.title: Exchange a foreign driving licence
records.description: Residents may be able to exchange a valid foreign driving licence for a Dutch licence. Eligibility depends on where the licence was issued and other conditions, and the application is submitted through the municipality.
records.ai_summary: Use the RDW eligibility rules to determine whether exchange is possible, then apply at the municipality with the required documents. If exchange is not permitted, Dutch theory and practical tests are normally required.
